For Medical Societies & Hospitals

A hospital performs at the level of its clinicians.

Individual digital gaps compound. When clinicians share a common framework — the same vocabulary, the same foundational concepts — workflows improve measurably. Think of driving: every driver has passed the same test, internalised the same rules. That shared standard is what makes traffic function. ODEON is that standard for digital health.

01
Baseline your clinicians

Deploy the DHSAT across your clinical staff. Results are aggregated and anonymised. No disruption to operations. A whole-institution baseline — by department, by role — in days.

02
Know where the risk is

Domain-level scores reveal where gaps are concentrated — cybersecurity, AI literacy, data governance. 82% of EU healthcare data breaches involve human error or undertrained clinicians. See exactly which teams need to act first.

03
Close the gaps systematically

Enrol cohorts into the relevant L1–L3 modules. CME-credited. Self-paced. Progress tracked and reported centrally. The performance of a clinical setting is the sum of its clinicians' performances.

🇪🇺 🇬🇷 HIMSS EMRAM · National Assessment
Like other EU countries, Greece has now assessed the digital maturity of its hospitals nationally via HIMSS EMRAM.

EMRAM scores reflect how digitally mature a hospital is as a system — how well it has adopted digital tools and processes at an organisational level. It measures the institution, not the clinicians working within it. A hospital can score highly on EMRAM and still have a clinical workforce that lacks the competencies to use those systems effectively. ODEON addresses the human gap in this equation.

What is HIMSS EMRAM? ↗

Institutional Analytics

"Know where your clinicians stand. Track how they improve."

As institutions enrol, ODEON builds Greece's first national dataset of clinical digital competence — and the first EU-level benchmark. Institutional reports are the documented evidence base for EU funding applications.

Mock data
Organisation dashboard — completion rate, domain scores, critical gaps
Mock data
Priority actions — why gaps matter and critical domain risks
Mock data
Staff breakdown by department & profession
Mock data
National capability overview — cross-organisation comparison
Mock data
Score heatmap & assessment status across organisations

Behind every institutional score is an individual clinician. The three reasons below explain what that means in practice.

What works at ward level scales to hospital level. What works at hospital level scales nationally. Greece's EMRAM results create a unique moment — the first time an entire country's hospital infrastructure has been measured against a single international standard. ODEON provides the clinical workforce layer that makes those results actionable.
Book an institutional briefing → or email info@odeonhealth.org

Institutional pathway covers Levels 1–3 · Enrolment opens Q1 2027

It all starts with clinicians

Three reasons clinicians need this now

Making technology work for you and your patients.

01

There's a measurable gap between clinicians who are confident with technology and those who aren't — and patients feel it.

The difference isn't talent or dedication. It's preparation. Clinicians who navigate digital systems fluently spend more time with patients, make fewer documentation errors, and engage more effectively with AI-assisted tools. Those who don't, compensate — and compensation has a cost.

Clinician digital competence is now a direct determinant of clinical outcomes. — WHO, OECD 2023
02

You're losing hours you can't afford to lose.

Avoidable friction with digital systems costs clinicians 3–5 hours every week. That's time in front of a screen instead of a patient — not because the tools are bad, but because the preparation never came.

EHR satisfaction gains from better preparation correlate with an 18.8% reduction in burnout risk. — Frontiers in Public Health 2025
03

The rules changed quietly. The accountability didn't.

New EU frameworks — on AI, data, and cybersecurity — have attached real professional obligations to individual clinicians. Most haven't been told. ODEON teaches the reasoning behind the rules, not the rules themselves.

All modules built around anonymised clinical scenarios · No IT jargon
Our mission

Why ODEON

Care quality enhanced by digitally capable clinicians. That's not a slogan. It's our metric. ODEON exists to close the gap between the tools clinicians are handed and the preparation they receive.

  • Built by practising clinicians — not vendors, not consultants
  • Outcomes-first: every module mapped to measurable competency change
  • Aligned with international digital health standards and European regulatory frameworks
  • CME accreditation: PIS (primary) · EACCME (international) — In progress
  • Greece first. European scale from 2028.

Greek non-profit (ΑΜΚΕ). No shareholder. No vendor agenda. Registered under G.E.MI. 194135838000.

Confirmed clinical partners
The founding team

The clinicians and technologists who decided to build the standard Europe's clinical workforce needed.

ODEON Academy

Five levels. One standard.
Built for practising clinicians.

Clinician-authored. CME-accredited. Built around how clinicians actually work.

Level 1
Formative

You understand the digital health landscape and your place within it.

3 modules CME-credited SAT entry

Institutional pathway · B2B

Level 2
Intermediate

You work confidently with digital tools and foundational data concepts.

4 modules CME-credited SAT entry

Institutional pathway · B2B

Level 3
Proficient

You apply digital health knowledge to improve care workflows.

5 modules CME-credited SAT entry

Institutional pathway · B2B

Level 4
Specialist Fellow

You lead digital health initiatives and contribute to standards.

CME-credited Exam Certification

Open to individual clinicians

Level 5
Senior Fellow

You shape strategy and advance the field at a systems level.

CME-credited Exam Certification

Open to individual clinicians

Specialist & Professional Tracks — Levels 4 & 5

Advanced practitioners choose a specialisation track for their examination pathway.

Level 4 — Specialist Fellow
4.1Interoperability & Standards
4.2Clinical Coding & Diagnostic Classification
4.3Telemedicine & Remote Care
4.4Clinical Data & AI Governance
4.5Clinical Decision Intelligence & Integrated Care
Level 5 — Senior Fellow
5.1Digital Health Strategy, Operations & Governance
5.2Health Tech Innovation & Assessment
How it works

A clinical pathway. Four steps. One credential that grows with you.

01
Assess

Take the free self-assessment. Six domains. Ten minutes. No preparation needed. Your result tells you exactly where to start.

Free · No sign-up
02
Learn

Your result maps to a level. Work through clinician-authored modules at your own pace — structured, practice-anchored, and CME-accredited.

CME-credited Self-paced
03
Earn your Credential

L1–L3: a stackable digital badge for each level, portable across Europe. L4–L5: sit the examination on your chosen specialist track and earn a named certification.

CME-credited L4–L5 Certified
04
Advance

Recertify every three years. Progress to the next level. Join the ODEON public register — a verifiable, searchable record of your digital health competence.

Public register 3-year renewal
Levels 1–3 · Institutional

Enrolment through your hospital, clinic, or medical association. Enrolment starts Q1 2027. Contact info@odeonhealth.org to express interest.

Levels 4–5 · Individual

Open directly to practising clinicians. Examination-based. Public register entry. Shareable post-nominal credential.

Our curriculum is developed by practising clinicians and reviewed by European digital health experts. Module details are shared with institutional partners upon engagement.

Free digital health self-assessment

Where do you stand?

Six domains. Ten minutes. No preparation. Your result maps directly to your Academy level.

1
Start the assessment

Click below. You are taken to the ODEON assessment platform — no account needed.

2
Work through each domain

Short, direct statements across six clinical domains. No wrong answers. Most finish in under 10 minutes.

3
Get your results instantly

Domain-by-domain scores and a suggested credential level — generated the moment you submit.

4
Receive your personal report

Enter your details once to unlock your full report and your Academy entry recommendation.

Calling Clinical Authors

Help shape Europe's first standardised digital health curriculum.

We are commissioning practising clinicians to author modules for the ODEON Academy. Selective. Peer-led. Your opportunity to define the standard.

What we're looking for
✓

Practising clinician or digital health expert — doctor, nurse, or allied health professional

✓

Minimum 10 years hands-on clinical and/or digital health experience

✓

European-based (preferred)

✓

Fluent English — ODEON handles all translations, dubbing, and subtitles

✓

Evidence-based, practice-first approach. No vendor promotion.

The commissioning process
01
Apply
Submit interest form
02
Review
CRB assessment & COI
03
Commission
Contract & brief
04
Deliver
Peer review & publish

Conflict of Interest declaration required. EACCME-aligned, 36-month lookback, vendor-specific exclusions. Reviewed by the ODEON Content Review Board.

What authors receive
€

€5,000 per module (incl. VAT) — fixed fee, exclusive rights, 3-year renewable contract
